channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: 'Like a microscopic Predator': Chinese scientists create tiny robotic vacuum to hunt radioactive pollution and clean the world's oceans
item.description: Chinese scientists created light-driven micromotors that actively capture uranium from water, offering a new approach to resource extraction challenges.

item.title: Tome, another Goodreads booktracker rival, shuts down
item.description: The app was one of several catering to the BookTok crowd.

item.title: Could Lovable’s automatic 10% pay raise be the cure for toxic cultures?
item.description: Stockholm-based vibe coding platform Lovable it is offering employees an automatic 10% raise as a way to sidestep classic corporate politics.

item.title: Amazon Luna's May lineup includes Guardians of the Galaxy and the Resident Evil 2 remake
item.description: Prime members can also claim a code for Mafia II: Definitive Edition.

item.title: How I Fixed My Webcam Lighting for Zoom Calls (2026)
item.description: I have a horrendous lighting setup in my home office, but as a mixed-use room, my options for fixing it were limited.

item.title: Hackers hack victims hacked by other hackers
item.description: An unknown group of hackers is breaking into systems previously breached by the cybercrime group TeamPCP. Once inside, the hackers immediately kick out TeamPCP and remove its hacking tools from the victims’ systems.

item.title: Meet ZAYA1-8B, a super efficient, open reasoning model trained on AMD Instinct MI300 GPUs
item.description: Even as leading AI providers like OpenAI and Anthropic battle over the compute to train and release ever larger, more powerful models, other labs are going in a different direction — pursuing the development of smaller, more efficient models and often open sourcing them. The latest worth paying attention to comes from the lesser-known Palo Alto startup Zyphra, which this week released its new reasoning, mixture-of-experts (MoE) language model, ZAYA1-8B, with just over 8 billion parameters and only 760 million active — far fewer than the trillions estimated for the likes of the big labs. Yet, Z...

item.title: Valve will open reservations on May 8 for the second wave of Steam Controllers
item.description: Hopefully fewer of the coveted controllers will be snapped up by bots and resellers this time.

item.title: Why cutting junior talent could backfire
item.description: There is a hidden cost of reducing headcount due to AI. Anyone spending time inside a company right now can feel it. There is a growing assumption shaping decisions at the highest levels. AI will drive efficiency and therefore companies are expected to reduce headcount.

item.title: Valve is fighting Steam Controller scalpers with a new reservations system
item.description: Valve's new Steam Controller sold out quickly when it went on sale on Monday, and the company is going to open a "reservations queue" on Friday at 1PM ET for anyone who missed that initial run. When the controller initially went on sale, many potential buyers experienced difficulties checking out before the controller went out […]

item.title: Kalshi doubles valuation in 5 months, hitting $22B
item.description: Prediction market startup Kalshi has raised a $1 billion Series F round led by Coatue.

item.title: ChatGPT’s ‘Trusted Contact’ will alert loved ones of safety concerns
item.description: OpenAI is launching an optional safety feature for ChatGPT that allows adult users to assign an emergency contact for mental health and safety concerns. Friends, family members, or caregivers designated as a "Trusted Contact" will be notified if OpenAI detects that a person may have discussed topics like self-harm or suicide with the chatbot. "Trusted […]...

item.title: What is the release date for Marshals: A Yellowstone Story episode 11 on CBS and Paramount+?
item.description: Last week, we jumped two weeks forward after the Cleggs brutally kidnapped Andrea. There's still lots left unsaid, so when does Marshals: A Yellowstone Story episode 11 hit CBS and Paramount+?

item.title: Tech spring cleaning: How declutter your devices and accounts
item.description: Here is a digital spring cleaning checklist. If the spring season has brought an urge to scrub your living space from top to bottom, why not clear out the digital detritus cluttering your electronic devices and online accounts at the same time?

item.title: Jeff Bezos rep leaves Slate Auto’s board
item.description: Melinda Lewison's departure raises questions about Bezos' support and involvement with the startup at a time when the Amazon founder is now apparently focused on robotics at his new startup, Project Prometheus.

item.title: Cozy city builder Town to City leaves early access on May 26
item.description: One of the genre's top-rated games is going 1.0 and adding a new map.

item.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
item.description: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead. Elon Musk, his financial […]

item.title: I’m a password expert – and these are my top five tips for picking the right password manager
item.description: Choosing a password manager doesn't have to be difficult, and I've shared my top five tips for making a good choice.

item.title: 'You would be able to say to it: 'Make a better version of yourself.' And it just goes off and does that completely autonomously': Anthropic Co-Founder on our wild 'recursive' AI future
item.description: Anthropic warns of AI capable of 'recursive self-improvement,' and we'd better prepare for this scary, near future.

item.title: A 60-yearlong analysis of nearly 800,000 workers found this to be the most stressful part of work
item.description: In a massive meta-analysis, researchers from three universities uncovered the biggest workplace stressors—with one very common problem in particular. American workers are stressed. Like, really stressed.

item.title: Spirit Airlines employees, adrift with no severance or healthcare, turn to GoFundMe after company collapse
item.description: The donation site now lists campaign after campaign from former captains, flight attendants, and ground staff. Spirit Airlines abruptly ended operations in the early morning on Saturday, May 2, following a failed government bailout for Spirit Aviation Holdings Inc.

item.title: Did Microsoft just tease a new Xbox UI?
item.description: Microsoft showed off a "consistent" Xbox UI across handhelds, consoles, and cloud gaming during its Xbox keynote at the Game Developers Conference in March. At the time it was difficult to see if there was anything new about the UI from the videos and photos captured during the event, but Microsoft has now given us […]

item.title: Taillé pour les créateurs et les monteurs, cet écran PC 32″ 6K avec ports Thunderbolt 5 perd 40 % de son prix
item.description: Avec sa grande dalle 6K et ses deux ports Thunderbolt 5, le moniteur LG UltraFine 32U990A-S est sans conteste un modèle ultramoderne, conçu pour les créateurs. Actuellement, LDLC l'affiche à 1 199,95 euros au lieu de 1 999,99 euros au lancement.

item.title: Tesla Model 3 : 600 000 km plus tard, voici ce qu’il reste de la batterie
item.description: Un YouTubeur canadien a racheté la Tesla Model 3 affichant le plus gros kilométrage en vente en Amérique du Nord : 609 828 km, batterie d'origine. Verdict du test : un tiers de capacité en moins, mais la voiture roule encore.

item.title: A new report says inkjet-printed OLED could be 30% cheaper to produce than current methods, which I'm hoping is great news for laptops and monitors soon, and OLED TVs in the future — and it's on top of the other big advantages of IJP tech
item.description: Inkjet-printed OLED panels could reduce manufacturing costs by as much as 35%, according to a new report. But there are still challenges to overcome

item.title: GrapheneOS patches an Android VPN bypass that Google decided to leave alone
item.description: A researcher disclosed an Android 16 VPN bypass that leaks your real IP. Google declined to patch it, but GrapheneOS shipped a fix within days.

item.title: ‘Our customers have been looking elsewhere for mobile’: BT relaunches BT Mobile for broadband customers, insisting it will ‘live alongside’ its existing EE network
item.description: BT is re-opening its BT Mobile service to existing broadband customers, saying its customers "have been looking elsewhere for mobile."

item.title: The Ploopy Bean is a travel-friendly mouse with a ThinkPad-style nub
item.description: No trackballs here.

item.title: Netflix teste sa propre recherche vocale IA sur la TV tout en snobant Gemini et Alexa
item.description: Netflix teste une recherche vocale dopée à l'IA, directement intégrée dans son app TV. Au passage, le service contourne Gemini et les assistants vocaux des téléviseurs connectés.

item.title: ‘Self-aggrandizing,’ ‘absolute horseshit’: Robert Downey Jr. has strong words for social media influencers
item.description: The ‘Iron Man’ actor said he doesn’t believe influencers will be the stars of the future, despite what younger generations may think. Few people know fame like Robert Downey Jr. The Oscar-winning actor has done everything from critical darlings like Oppenheimer to pop culture juggernauts like The Avengers.

item.title: J’ai testé la Sonos Play : l’enceinte portable nomade qui promet le meilleur des deux mondes
item.description: Salon, terrasse ou jardin : la Sonos Play confirme le virage nomade des enceintes Sonos. Longtemps cantonnées à un usage domestique en Wi-Fi, elles gagnent désormais en liberté grâce à une batterie intégrée et une conception pensée pour suivre l’utilisateur partout. Le son Sonos est toujours au rendez-vous.
